title: Race-lap query ranker with top-k pair construction
summary: This is naturally a within-lap ordering problem: at a given race-lap only a few drivers are realistic pit candidates.
rationale: This is naturally a within-lap ordering problem: at a given race-lap only a few drivers are realistic pit candidates. Learning-to-rank methods explicitly optimize ordering within query groups and public XGBoost/CatBoost docs support groupwise objectives that may capture this structure better than plain binary classification.
implementation_hint: Use `qid = Year_Race_LapNumber`, train an XGBoost `rank:ndcg` expert with `lambdarank_pair_method=topk` and small top-k, and/or a CatBoost ranking expert (`QueryCrossEntropy` or `YetiRankPairwise`). Blend its OOF ranks with the existing classifier ensemble rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Should add a distinct model view that improves separation inside true pit windows, especially when absolute probabilities are noisy but relative ordering within a lap is strong.
risk: Many query groups may have few effective positive pairs, so ranking-only models can be unstable unless grouped carefully and blended with a classifier.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
